findings.title: No src/ structure — acceptable for skeleton, flag for future epics
findings.description: The Nuxt 4 skeleton contains only three files at the frontend/ root: app.vue, nuxt.config.ts, and package.json. No src/components, src/services, src/config directories exist. For a skeleton task with no business logic this is correct, but the infrastructure-setup convention requires a src/-based separation of concerns for web apps (src/components, src/services, src/lib, src/config) before meaningful feature work begins.
findings.impact: If the next epic adds components and services directly to frontend/ root without establishing src/ structure, it becomes harder to refactor later as the codebase grows.
findings.recommendation: Create the standard Nuxt 4 src/ folder structure (src/components/, src/pages/, src/composables/, src/services/, src/config/) before or during the first feature epic that adds real UI code. This aligns with the infrastructure-setup skill convention for web apps.

findings.title: Gitleaks pre-commit hook not installed for Task 3
findings.description: The .git/hooks/ directory contains only sample files — no active pre-commit hook. The gitleaks hook is deferred to Task 6 (scripts/install-hooks.sh). This is by design in the task decomposition, but it means the Task 3 commit (frontend skeleton + pnpm-lock.yaml) was made without gitleaks protection. The pnpm-lock.yaml file is 220 KB and could theoretically contain embedded secrets from malicious packages, though this is a low-probability risk for official nuxt 4.4.4.
findings.impact: Any commits made before Task 6 is completed are not scanned for secrets. Low risk for the current skeleton but establishes a gap window.
findings.recommendation: This is acceptable given the task decomposition design. Ensure Task 6 is completed before any task that handles credentials, tokens, or environment configuration. Document the gap in the task 6 ticket if not already noted.

findings.title: *.lock pattern in .gitignore sits above project-specific sections
findings.description: Line 37 of .gitignore contains '*.lock' under the 'System' section. This pattern matches files with a .lock extension (e.g. Gemfile.lock, composer.lock) but does NOT match pnpm-lock.yaml (which has a .yaml extension). The pnpm-lock.yaml is correctly tracked in git (confirmed in commit 66193bb). However, the generic *.lock pattern is ambiguous — a developer adding a Gradle .lock file or similar could be confused about whether it is intentionally excluded. Additionally, future package managers that generate *.lock files (not *.yaml) would be unexpectedly gitignored.
findings.impact: Low risk currently. pnpm-lock.yaml is correctly committed. However the *.lock glob could accidentally exclude legitimate lock files in future tooling.
findings.recommendation: Either remove the generic '*.lock' pattern and replace with specific exclusions if any are needed, or add a comment clarifying its intent. Add an explicit '!frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml' negation pattern as a safety net to ensure it is always tracked regardless of future .gitignore changes.
